D8 AnthropologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Berlin-Kay 11-Step Colour Term Evolution

Berlin & Kay (1969) showed colour terms enter a language in a near-universal order — black/white → red → green/yellow → blue → brown → purple/pink/orange/grey

B&K surveyed 98 languages and found basic colour terms emerge in fixed stages. Stage I: only "warm/light" vs "cool/dark". Stage II: + red. Stage III: + green or yellow. Stage IV: both. Stage V: + blue. Stage VI: + brown. Stage VII: + purple/pink/orange/grey. The ordering is strict — no language has "blue" without also having "red".

Drag stage; current language vocabulary expands across the 11-term universe.
